South Korea's highest-paid math tutor makes $14 million a year, more than Son Heung-min, their most famous footballer. His name is Hyun Woo-jin. He graduated from Stanford's math department in three years and three months. His textbook series, Neuron, sells over a million copies a year. Every November, South Korea pauses for a single nine-hour exam called the Suneung. The Air Force halts training flights. For 35 minutes during the English listening section, no commercial flights can take off or land at Incheon, one of Asia's busiest airports. About 8,000 police officers escort late students on motorcycles with sirens running. Inside that exam, the most consequential questions are called killer questions. They're deliberately designed to fall outside the public school curriculum. They exist to separate the top one percent from everyone else, because that one percent gets into Seoul National, Korea, and Yonsei. The SKY universities that produce around 30% of Korea's top-1,000-company CEOs. Parents can't evaluate the curriculum themselves. They can't reverse-engineer the killer question. So they borrow Hyun Woo-jin's judgment. If your child went through Neuron and still failed, you did what every reasonable parent in the neighborhood does. The fault is your child's, or fate's. Not yours. That's what's actually being sold. Not math. Certainty. Korean households spent 29.2 trillion won on private tutoring in 2024, an all-time record, even as the school-age population dropped another 1.5%. A country is, in a measurable sense, choosing not to exist in order to keep paying for that certainty.

What my Puppy Pampers look like with the gorgeous little star model @BaileyandFam 🥹 One thing I forgot to mention is that every Puppy Pamper is completely tailored to the puppy and built up gradually over their monthly sessions. The very first session is often just a bath, brush and blowdry to help them feel safe, relaxed and confident in the salon environment. For puppies that will eventually need haircuts, I’ll slowly introduce little bits such as trimming around the eyes, feet or hygiene areas, but only when I know they’re comfortable and happy with it 🥰 Sometimes it takes a few sessions of just bathing and drying before we move onto anything more. I’ll never push a puppy to do more than they’re ready for and my main goal with the sessions is to make the grooming process as positive and enjoyable for them as possible. I recommend Puppy Pampers as soon as they are covered by their vaccinations 🥰 #goldenretriever #puppy #doggrooming #doggroomer #fyp

bakery style oreo stuffed cookies 🍪🥛    recipe: ingredients: ¾ cup salted butter, softened (170 g) ¾ cup packed brown sugar (150 g) ¼ cup granulated sugar (50 g) 1 large egg  1 tsp vanilla (5 g) 2 cups all-purpose flour (240 g) 1 tbsp cornstarch (8 g) ½ tsp baking soda (3 g) ¼ tsp salt (1.5 g) oreos, some whole some  roughly chopped  white chocolate (measure with your heart)  & choceur cookie and cream chocolate’s   instructions: cream the butter and sugars together  until smooth and slightly fluffy. mix in the egg and vanilla until fully combined. in a separate bowl, whisk together the flour, cornstarch, baking soda, and salt. fold the dry ingredients into the wet just until combined.  chop some of the oreos into small chunks ( leave some for topping ) and fold them in along with the white chocolate. scoop the dough into 8 portions, flatten and add an oreo inside and chocolate then shape into tall mounds. ( i flatten the top but keep them tall ) bake at 375°F for 11–14 minutes, until the edges are set and the centers are still soft. ( baking time may vary ) let cool to set the cookies!  i added moser roths white chocolate to the bottom & inside !    #oreo #cookies #chocolatechipcookies #Recipe #baking

Are they really back? Yes, absolutely. We exclusively tested both new Mercurial models in Madrid (thanks to @Nike Football and @hubcommnet), as always, we want to give you every possible detail to build your own opinion: visuals, product insights, but most importantly our real feedback after using them on pitch, in matches and during specific training sessions that seriously “stressed” both boots. The new Superfly sends a very clear message as soon as you put it on: innovation built to deliver maximum propulsion. That’s exactly what the forefoot “plate” gives you, an aggressive sense of push and energy return that you can genuinely feel while sprinting. Vapor changes too and the almost “strange” feeling when wearing it for the first time is actually maximum comfort. Not because Mercurial Vapor has never been comfortable, but comfort has probably never been the first thing that comes to mind when thinking about this boot. Other details you’ll definitely want to know: Vapor is the lightest Mercurial ever made at just 130g, while Superfly features a very grippy upper despite being a product fully focused on speed and sprinting. Sizing? Stay relaxed with your usual Nike size, this is 100% true to size. We have a lot more content directly filmed in Madrid coming very soon, so as always, feel free to leave your questions in the comments. This routine is for anyone who is not dealing with extreme damage but wants over all glossier & softer hair this is the the exact routine I built for getting glass hair on fine hair but will work for the wavy curly hair girls too!!  the science: the flatten your cuticle lies, the more light it reflects that's what gives the illusion of glass. glossy hair isn't a product, it's the consistency of routine over time that’s keeping that cuticle smooth from prewash to final styling  it took me a while to figure out the system, but once I did, the results compounded and now I wanna share my favourite tips with you because this is the routine that actually for every  wash day. the 5 things I never skip: 1. always create a barrier before shampoo: hair oil or conditioner first. less friction = less cuticle damage = more shine 2. weekly hair mask. deep hydration is what keeps that cuticle flat and the shine consistent. 3. a glossing treatment every 3-4 weeks  this is the step that takes glossy to glass  4. leave-in conditioner every single wash. beats frizz and keeps the cuticle flat between washes. 5. the hair sandwich method oil before heat styling AND after. then always finish with a cool shot to lock the cuticle in place ✨ Hope these haircare tips help you guys!! #hair #haircare #glasshair #hairgloss

So fluffy & delicious!🤎 Recipe below👇🏼  🍩 Ingredients Dough: * 300g strong white bread flour * 30g caster sugar * 1/2 tsp salt * 7g fast-action yeast * 2 eggs * 75ml warm milk * 50g melted butter Chocolate Pastry Cream: * 540g whole milk * 2 tsp vanilla extract * 18g cocoa powder * 2 eggs * 2 egg yolks * 100g brown sugar * 25g cornstarch * ¼ tsp sea salt * 170g milk chocolate, chopped * 30g butter 🧾Instructions 1. Make the Dough Mix flour, sugar, salt, and yeast in a bowl. Add eggs, warm milk, and melted butter. Knead until elastic by hand or in mixer. Cover and let rise for 1 hour. 2. Shape & Proof Punch down the dough and divide into 65g portions. Roll each piece into a smooth ball and place onto individual squares of baking paper. Arrange on a tray, cover loosely, and let rise for another hour.  3. Make Chocolate Cream Heat milk and vanilla until steaming. In a bowl, whisk cocoa, eggs, yolks, sugar, cornstarch, and salt. Slowly add the hot milk while whisking constantly, then return to pan and cook until thick & bubbling. Off heat, stir in chocolate and butter. Chill with plastic wrap touching the surface. 4. Fry the Donuts Fry donuts in hot oil. Fry until golden. Drain and roll in sugar. 5. Fill Transfer the chilled chocolate pastry cream to a piping bag fitted with a round nozzle. Poke a small hole in the side of each donut and pipe in the filling generously. Ooey-Gooey Scoopable Nutella Cookie Pie🤤 Yields: 15-16 Chunky Cookies INGREDIENTS:  230g Cold Chopped Unsalted Butter  160g Caster Sugar  160g Light Brown Sugar  450g Chocolate Chunks or chips  475g Plain Flour  1 tsp Salt 3 tsp Baking Powder  3/4 tsp Baking Soda  2 Eggs  1 Egg Yolk  1 tsp Vanilla  INSTRUCTIONS: •Cut cling wrap into small squares. Scoop ½ teaspoon Nutella onto individual cling-wrap squares. Fold to seal and freeze until solid. (Repeat for however many Nutella stuffed cookies you plan to make.) •In a large mixing bowl, add the cold cubed butter and whisk until slightly creamy. Add the white sugar and light brown sugar, then whisk together until well combined and fluffy. •Add the eggs, vanilla extract, and chocolate chips or chunks. Whisk until fully incorporated. •Add the flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Gently mix until just combined, do not overmix. •Portion the dough into 90g balls. Press a frozen Nutella piece into the centre of each dough ball, seal well, and roll into a smooth ball. •Place the stuffed cookie dough balls onto a tray and freeze for at least 30 minutes (or overnight for best results). •Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F). •Take any shape baking dish circle, square, rectangle anything and line it with parchment paper. Arrange frozen cookie dough balls in it leaving some space between them. •Bake for 10-15 minutes (timing depends on the type of pan) or until the tops are lightly golden while the centres remain soft and gooey. •Remove from the oven and let cool slightly. •Serve warm with ice cream and chocolate sauce enjoy!✨ NOTE: Store any extra dough balls in an airtight container or freezer bag and freeze for later. Whenever a cookie craving hits, bake straight from frozen🫶🏻 #cookie #nutella #cookiepie #fyp #viral

Add the white sugar and light brown sugar, then whisk together until well combined and fluffy. •Add the eggs, vanilla extract, and chocolate chips or chunks. Gently mix until just combined, do not overmix. •Portion the dough into 75-80g balls. •Place the cookie dough balls onto a tray and freeze for at least 30 minutes (or overnight for best results). •Preheat oven to 165-170C. Its all abt consistency!!🖤 But here's my routine💕 -wash my hair 3-4times a week.  -shampoo only focus on my scalp (not to my length).  -shampoo sles and non sls (like 2 times non sls and 1 time sles, its for balance my scalp n length) -always use conditioner in every hairwashday -hairmask 2times a week (after shampoo when ur scalp alrdy clean, on wet hair not dry) -always use heat protect (spray serum or cream) -also took some suplement d3 n zincplus -always trim my splits end every month for 1-2cm -always massage my scalp for 10min  -hair oiling 2-3times a week, im using rosemary based for hair growth. -scalp serum once a day.  -scrunchie and hair silk bonnet for sleep. -eat more protein and sleep enough. #hairtransformation #hairgrowth #longhair
